text:
Worst interview of my life | A 5 panel person interview just ripped into me with the force of Moses parting the Red Sea. Totally flubbed on the technical questions, shredded like a piece of paper. This has me reconsidering if analytics is even the field for me. 

To everyone who has been destroyed in interviews I now completely understand how devastating it is. It was so bad that the initial manager who screened me strongly emphasized to his coworkers that he had other candidates ready to be interviewed. 

I sent a message apologizing to him but I’ll likely never hear from them again. And that’s for the best. What a disaster

Fired after only 1 month. 3rd time being terminated. | As the title states, I was let go today from my job of barely a month as a business operations associate at an engineering firm. This isn’t the first time I’ve been terminated but a month seems quite harsh.

I have mixed feelings. While I didn’t particularly care for the job itself it was still good experience and the pay and benefits were high. I was hoping to stay at least 18 months but that’s no longer possible. In addition, my unemployment durations vary. There were times I’ve found another full time job within a few weeks to more recently it took over 3 years to land a FT position after dozens of failed interviews and numerous crappy temporary minimum wage work. I won’t survive going through such an ordeal again.

I was told the reason for termination was because I wasn’t understanding the duties quickly enough but no one becomes proficient in a job in only a few weeks unless it’s basic unskilled labor. I will admit though I do have ADD and thus I take a much longer time to learn and tend to struggle with detailed oriented tasks for which this role was extremely detailed oriented (absolutely everything had to be perfect and accurate since we’re of course an engineering firm). 

However, I’m not sure where to go from here. Practically every non-management job relies on attention to detail and I frankly can’t handle minutiae, repetitive tasks without mentally shutting down (brain fog, low productivity, making silly errors etc) and my skill set isn’t suitable for freelancing. I’ve mainly worked administrative, project coordination and business ops roles. Any advice or possible jobs that might suit me?

About me: 27M, no degree, live at home, good with coming up with ideas, bad at details.

I’m two weeks into a new job— and I HATE it.... advice? Open to hearing similar stories so I feel better. | I started a new job and it’s been two full weeks of work. I was super excited about this job and now that I’m into it, I’m miserable. It’s nothing like I thought it was going to be, it seems like everyone is a machine, and there isn’t the type of team work I expected there to be based off the interview conversation. 

I feel like I was so blindsided by this whole job and feel so stupid at how I took this job expecting something “better”. I’ve started to actively look for new work. I just feel so bad or it feels wrong to leave a job so early. This job is completely remote, they mailed me the things I need like a laptop, additional screen, keyboard and mouse, headset. So having all of that and then leaving early just does NOT make me feel good. 

I’ve cried so much, have had constant anxiety to the point where I can’t even think properly. I’m used to working, it has nothing to do with being lazy. The job itself has sucked. The training was bad, I feel SO confused still, it hasn’t gotten better, and I don’t feel like I can grow at all with this job. To give more details: It’s full time contract, no benefits, and not amazing pay (only $18/hr). I’m turning 22 years old this year and I already have 2 years of experience in similar work. My previous job (which I LOVED and miss so much) ended because I was on a student contract and they didn’t have the budget to take me on as a regular employee when I graduated.

I’ve received advice from people in my life but I still have so many mixed emotions. My close friend told me being new in the workforce and hating a job is normal and I should just quit, someone else said that i should keep working until another job comes up, someone else said it’ll get better— I TRULY do not think this is the job for me, I’m not sure how else to justify it. I’m someone who values my work-life-balance and I feel like this job is totally throwing me off and making me hate waking up each day. I went from being excited to work to wanting to call in sick because of how bad my anxiety is.

I just want to add, my previous job I had the classic new job anxiety but I quickly overcame it and ended up loving the job and the people. So far, I feel NOTHI like that for this job. This is more than new job anxiety....

Help!!!!!

Has anyone started their own business/ went self-employed? How did you do it and how do you find it? | Thinking about maybe one day going down these routes and looking for opinions on the difference between working for a company and working for yourself/ starting your own business/ going freelance.

Any advice is appreciated.

Boss asking me to do things that aren't my job | So today my boss told me to clean the break room. Mind you, this place is absolutely disgusting. It looks like it hasn't been cleaned in years. Mouse poop everywhere that's been sitting there since I started a year ago. Grime built up everywhere.  It was like that when I first started there. Of course they didn't show me it when I started there. I found out how nasty it was after I started. When my boss told me to clean it today I said, "that's not what I signed up for and you guys make enough money, pay someone to clean it." I also said if they paid me I would clean it. He looked at me like in shock. I was kind of shocked that came out of my mouth too. I'm sick of him walking all over me. I've been standing up for myself lately and he doesn't like it.

 I don't know why I'm asked to clean it when none of it is my mess. I work in a office and I do paperwork it isn't my job to clean up after other people. Also why only me why can't other people help ? Everyone is sitting around doing nothing yet I'm asked to do everything. Their too cheap to hire someone to do it. It pisses me off.


Do you think I should clean it ? I'm fixing my resume up and applying for jobs but I don't want to get fired. I want to quit on my own terms. Also do you think I'm being unreasonable?

Not sure if I was set up to fail or just a bad junior developer | I started as a junior developer at this small e-commerce company after graduating in MIS. 6 months later they put me in charge as the sole developer for building a brand new e-commerce website on a Magento 2 instance using LAMP stack (html/css/JavaScript/jQuery/php).

I had little help as my manager is extremely busy and had little to no oversight. I also was the company’s IT help desk, but that didn’t take too much of my time.
This new website had around 100 products that were complex manufacturing machines, each requiring custom product pages. I had to build all new CMS pages often requiring jQuery/JavaScript, and several small custom backend functionality’s in PHP.
The project is pushed live as it still fulfills most requirements, but is around 85% completely finished.

Frankly, I’m ashamed that this took me a year to get up to that point from the date it started. I feel that I could have done better. I’ve read that complex e-commerce sites usually take 3 months to complete. According to my software dev friends I was set up to fail, but I’d like to hear what some of y’all think.

If you dislike your job, make it a (relatively) smaller part of your life. | Seeing a ton of posts about devs dreading waking up and working in the morn. It's going to get worse as things open up and people start enjoying themselves if you're stuck inside all day. If you feel that CS has you feeling down, tied to a screen for 8+ hours a day, take some time to think about how accurate your perception of most jobs is. Are you in a self-fulfilling prophecy? 

This subreddit is _focused_ on CS careers. Don't let it convince you that successful engineers do nothing but code all day and browse subreddits and engineering blogs all night. They develop other parts of their lives with as much intention and energy as they put into their careers.

Having life outside of CS - friends outside of work who don't work in tech, family, hobbies, etc - is very beneficial to a productive 40 year career. 

For most people anyway. There are some people for whom it's just not "work".

The physical modeling aspect sounds cool, but I'd like to be on the development side eventually. This seems like it could be a good way to get into the field, I'm just a bit scared I'll get stuck in a QE role.

Anyone have any thoughts? Thanks!

The amount of fake job listings on indeed and LinkedIn are ridiculous and annoying | Basically title says it all. It is so disheartening to apply to a job or see a job listing, and then realize the company is not even real. There are so many fake companies that use the names of actual established companies to confuse and scam us.
I applied to a communications job and truthfully did not research the company in the moment. I got a call back today and was asked to interview tomorrow on zoom. I said okay and looked back at the job listing to practice my answers and think of any questions I had. 
I looked at their LinkedIn profile and it seemed strange, no postings or anything. I looked at their website and it was very very basic, and gave little to no substantial information. I also could not find the company address or phone number. Not listed in the BBB or anywhere really. When I looked at their Instagram, I realized this was for a fact a MLM scheme. They were tagged in another ‘marketing’ companies post speaking about creating new branches and what not, and had a bunch of young kids tagged. Its so annoying and im so tired of all these MLM schemes getting us excited just to turn out to be door to door sales and wastes of time.

If you are in the South Florida area avoid these companies, as they are all MLM schemes:
The Home Run Place, Florida Business Consulting, The Linum Group, MK Revolution, Otown Citi, Market Revolution, Compass Miami Co, The Sulit Group

And unfortunately im the sure the list goes on and on and on

Can I just Vent About another job Rejection? | It is hard not to take it personally but this is the third time I have been through the final round to only be sent a generic letter..."Sorry but we have chosen to go with another candidate."  I put so much effort into prepping and I felt the least I could of gotten was, " hay you didn't make it but,,,,,,"  Everyone gets so philosophical oh maybe it was not your perfect fit etc but people I have a family to feed.  And as for those people (recruiters and hiring managers) that treat you like dirt just want to say let's hope that you don't land up on the other side of the job searching market.  Thank you for letting me VENT got to go back to my job searching platforms and figure out why these people don't like me enough to give me a chance.

Is Web Development a good starting point in a career for later rotation to positions such as Data Scientist, Cybersecurity Engineer, etc. Or is it better to concentrate on something specific that I want to do in the future? | Hey guys, I'm a CS student, and I spent a lot of time thinking which career path to take.  


I started thinking on the other side, getting a job in Data Science, Cyber Security, etc is very difficult without further advanced education and experience.  
So I decided to go down the road of web or mobile development to start with. And then, when I finish my education, with experience, try to get a job as a data scientist, for example

Is it a good decision? or is it better to concentrate on something specific that I want to do in the future?
Is there a job that pays $400-$500 a week just working online completely ?

Don’t grill people about their availability | I had a lady call me up and ask if I was still interested in coming in for an interview for a place that I applied to a few months back and I said yes. We then proceeded to try and find a date and time for me to come in and she asks if I’m available to come in on Monday. I told her that I was working that day but I was available X Y and Z date next week. She asked me what time I work Monday and I told her afternoons for shift relief and closing. She then asked me about Monday morning and I told her that I’m not available in the mornings (I had also made it clear in my cover letter that I was not available in the mornings, that my availability is afternoons and early evenings). She then starts grilling me about why I’m not available. I told her that my husbands business is just getting back up on its feet again now that covid restrictions are lifting in our area and that we are still waiting for one more employee to come back from their leave. so he needs me in the mornings. She then barks at me “Well what are you going to do when you get a full time job?”

“We would adjust my schedule in his store accordingly.” Apparently that wasn’t the “right” answer as I got a very flippant, snarky “fine we will get back to you.”

Honestly, I should have told her that it’s none of her business and left it at that. Instead I told her the truth and she couldn’t handle it. 

For all you young people, don’t ever let anyone try to guilt you or grill you about what your availability is and why it is what it is. If a boss refuses to respect that as early on as the interview process, walk. The job is not worth it and you deserve better. They showed you their true colours right off the bat. I’m turning 31 in the new year and it took me until my mid to late 20’s to figure out that not only do I not deserve that kind of grief, but to actually learn what it’s like to work for a good boss that respects their employees. Once I got a taste of that, I was permanently hooked. 


Never ever allow someone to convince you that you need to light yourself on fire to keep someone else warm.

My mental health took a bad turn lately and I have to put that over my career at the moment. I’m 26 now and I will need few months before getting back to everyday life. Is it too late to have a career or how do I explain the gap to employers? | I want to give it a bit of a background. I graduated from university in October 2018 and had a mix of marketing and freelancing jobs. I quickly realized I didn’t want to do just marketing or freelance. After my last freelance work I wanted to get back to corporate and took to customer service role. First one was just a plan to get back and the latter was the only one I could find due to pandemic. My goal was to go back to school to get the training I need to get the career I want. Anyways once my contract ended i became really depressed and lately I got diagnosed with OCD. I also want to point out I was diagnosed with anxiety when I was 18, and due to my arrogance as a young adult and just wanting to get a job I always neglected proper treatment that often time I would have set backs due to my anxiety and made doing things extra difficult. 

Flash forward to today, it got so bad that now I have to check into day hospital and started taking medications. It’s not that I don’t wanna job, I badly want to work, but now doing the simplest thing taking me a lifetime and the last thing I want is to get a job, and then get fired for not being able to perform to my own standard and then get more depressed. So it’s vital for me to get better first. So I am asking is it too late for me to have a career. My plan is to recover within the next few months and then either do a masters or another bachelor abroad where there’s a better chance for the career I want to work at. But first I want to get better like well, so I don’t have this reoccurring problem.
How do you explain to the interviewer that you spent 2 years recovering from depression?

So i accepted a job and have worked there for a month but i got an email... | I got an email today from a place i previously interviewed at that they’re doing interviews for their March hiring class and they’d like me to interview again. I accepted a job in January doing flight and ground dispatching for an organ and tissue donation non profit. The thing is i already accepted and worked here for a few month. The job is emotionally draining though coordinating transportation of donors to recovery and planning organ flights and such. I just don’t enjoy working with so much death. It can be sad but rewarding too because you know what you’re doing is saving lives. What should I do? The other job pays $10k more a year and is more in sync with the kind of career i want. How do i answer the question “so why are you looking for a job already”

"We will help you succeed but if you do not perform up to our rigorous expectations we will let you go", is it a red flag to hear something like that on my "offer meeting"? | So I recently received a very competitive offer from a series-B startup without a lot of online presence on glassdoor, etc. to research on (just  a handful of reviews which sounds fine). When I had my final interview with the manager to announce the offer to me, he mentioned that the company is a place to learn and make mistakes and they want to help me to succeed but at the same time they expect fast delivery of results and if they see I'will struggle to deliver the results they will let me go.

I am a recent graduate and actually a hard-working and passionate person but talking about "letting me go" before even joining the company put a bad taste in my mouth tbh. I am not very experienced so maybe it is normal and appreciated to be forward with candidates like that and maybe I need to develop a thicker skin and not be as sensitive. There were a few other things that made me feel a little bit icky like no one in this company **ever** started their emails with a greeting or anything like that. like if your name is john, the email will be like "John, please see the attachment regarding your offer." I feel kinda childish writing this but I don't know why I don't receive a good vibe from my interactions with them. Like I feel more nervous than excited about joining the company...

Again, maybe I am over sensitive so I'd like to hear other opinions.
My recruiter stole my idea and code

Was confident I was going to be made an offer, but was told that they’re no longer interested. The reason is something they weren’t transparent about, do I reply and let them know? | Hi Reddit, before I start this, I want to preface with saying that I don’t think that this potential employer owes me anything, I am just really annoyed at the way things were handled. 

I went through two interviews for a role that I think I am a perfect fit for. After the second interview, I followed up and was asked for references. I provided them immediately, and then was ghosted for about a week. I followed up last week asking what’s going on, and got an email this morning stating that they have no doubt that I can do the job and would be a great fit, but are no longer interested because of other candidates and salary requirements. The language of the response leads me to believe that they don’t want to move forward because my salary requirements were too high. However, this was not communicated to me when I spoke with the hiring manager about my requirements, and there was no mention from them that that was too high, or asked me if I would be flexible. Which, I would have been more than happy to discuss and be flexible because I felt it was the right opportunity. The way that they’ve handled this is a bit of a red flag, but this is a very unique role and I think that it is an extremely good fit and some thing I’ve been looking for for a long time. I also forfeited another potential offer because they had this company led me to believe that I was a shoo-in for the role and now all of the sudden I’m not being considered anymore because of salary. When he asked me, I just gave him the highest number that I would be looking for.  

I’m looking for advice as to whether I should reply and say that I’m disappointed to hear this because had I known that my salary requirement was on the high end, I would’ve been happy to discuss what their specific range was for the role, but they gave me no indication there was any problem. I understand from a business point of view, if they find someone who’s also a good fit with a lower salary requirements, they are going to do that, but I’m just pissed that they wasted my time and weren’t transparent with me. Is it worth replying and saying that, or should I just thank them for their time and let it go?

Edit: I replied just thanking him and let it go. For context since some people are being accusatory in their responses, the first line of my post states that I don’t think this potential employer owes me anything. I just feel as though they should have been transparent that what I was looking for was higher than they were looking to pay. Again, for my privacy and not to give any specific details, I didn’t share the exact verbiage of the message, but they basically had said they know I can do the job and am a great fit but due to salary requirements decided to go in another direction. This, to me, meant it was my salary requirements were the issue for them. Which is fine, then it’s not a good fit, just felt like they could’ve been transparent about their range for the role and communicated with me to see if I was willing to negotiate (which I would’ve been happy to negotiate total compensation because I really liked the role).

Worked my a*s for this job: first week and I’m checked out… Is this normal? | I just graduated from university and got into consulting. It was a big deal for me due to consulting’s recruitment process (target schools, grades, etc.). I worked my tail off to get this job and I’ve beat the odds to get here. In all honesty, this is a stepping stone to other fields I’m targeting but still something I hustled to get.

First week started and I’m mentally checked out. It’s remote/virtual and I have no interest in our work except maybe a few niche project areas. No interest in socials, taking initiative during our training sessions, etc.

Is this normal? I feel like I should be in the honeymoon phase, radiating pride, enthusiasm and curiosity. All I’m thinking off right now is exit opportunities…

EDIT: For those too lazy to read the comments, here are what people are saying:

It's normal, as no job is perfect and especially not your first job out of school!
Give it a sincere shot and leave after no less than six months unless it's absolutely detestable.
Remote work is really hard for young/new hires so cut yourself some slack.

It looks like I'll be staffed with a team that specializes in a field which I love! I'll stick around and give it a sincere effort for a few months and reevaluate afterwards!
:)

Anyone else just decline a job because it took so long | Declining a job because they took so long to get back to you? This company interviewed me and during the interview surprised me with the “we aren’t really remote and will need you in office twice a week.” Not only that but the office is an hour from where I live. They told me they are interested in another interview and will follow up and will negotiate higher salary.

Well every week they update me with “we are still interested and will follow up to schedule the second interview.” It’s been almost 2 months now. I think I’m just going to tell them I’m no longer interested. 

Anyone else experience something like this? This seems like a huge red flag.

Why do employers advertise a salary band if they are only willing to pay the lowest point on the band. | I’ve just been offered a job which had a salary band of £23,000 to £32,000. I knew that it would be not at the top of the band, which would be a salary decrease for me but the job is what I want to be doing, but I didn’t expect there to be no negotiation from the bottom of the band. Why advertise a band if you won’t come up from the bottom. Why waste people’s time.
Life will get better

Some get to the final round to be disqualified. Others never respond or respond 9 months later. It takes a toll on your mental health when you feel useless sometimes or no one wants to hire you. Junior Dev - I'm being told everywhere(including reddit and my job) to ask questions, but whenever I do, I'm being met with unnecessary hostility that makes me feel like an idiot. What do? Is this normal? | So I'm working in this company for 7 months now. For the most part it has been great until I've been moved to a project in an external company(feeling like a freelancer...) I've been given a large, high priority task in an extremely abstract and  complex application with unrealistic deadlines.

I struggle, I struggle hard and even though I'm doing my best, the requirements either change, are not mentioned during meetings, or overcomplicate things to the point of me having to plan the structure for days. My problem is that I basically have noone to ask for help, Im alone on this, I have 0 guidance and I keep getting behind.

To be truthful help/guidance has been offered to me numerous times, but whenever I actually ask for it it goes something like this:

Me: how should the relationship between A and B affect C? 
SDev:  Why didn't you ask me for D?
Me: What about D?
SDev: wooooow, well that sucks, what have you been doing all that time exactly? Look it up and get to work we have a deadline.

Me: gets extremely anxious, spends hours on looking for a relation between A,B,C and D which turns out to not even exist.

0 progress for an entire day. Next day manager knocks on the door asking about my progress. 

Notice that my original question was never actually answered. It's an endless cycle of anxiety and feeling like a failure.

I'm absolutely terrified of asking questions to my manager and senior dev's because everyone seems to think "he probably is slacking off all day, that's why he doesn't know anything, lemme make him feel bad about that" rather than "let's answer this question real quick, the feature will be done faster and everyone will be happy".

A week ago I've almost had a panic attack before weekly meeting because I always have to explain the reasons behind my failure, instead of showing successfully developed feature or at least a significant progress.

I feel slow, anxious, burnt out, terrified of meetings, scared of going to work.

I'm also extremely anxious about being fired, it's an extremely hard situation due to the pandemic and finding a new job could take quite a while. I earn very little(I can save like $100 a month). I feel defeated.

Do you guys have any advice on what to do in a situation like this?
AMA with Rob Barnett, the author of Next Job, Best Job.

Father thinks there's something wrong with my resume/CV instead of the job market. | Despite the ongoing pandemic and the subsequent effects on the economy, after 180+ applications, my father is getting irritated because I "should have found a job by now."  I already had my resume optimized by a professional and still no positive news. Its becoming extremely frustrating dealing with him insinuating im not trying at all.

Worry that I'm gonna get fired soon | I'm a new grad starting my first job out of college 2 months ago. The company I work at is a well established company. However, I feel like onboarding has been lacking. I only have 1:1 with my mentor once every week. Just a week ago, he announced to the team that he'd be leaving the company. I'm now swimming all by myself. 

So far the team has assigned me with some tickets to work on, but I keep running into bugs and blocker that it keeps delaying my code being pushed out. I really want to ask for help but with WFH, some times I find it difficult to show a bug with enough context over messages. My coworkers also seem like they have a lot on their plates to actually help me out. 

I currently sit on a ticket that was assigned to me 2 sprints ago. Last sprint I thought I would probably have those tickets done quickly in this sprint but I continue to run into new bugs and errors as the code gets deploy. I didn't think that such trivial tickets would get delayed that much. Not only that, being young and naive, last sprint when asked during sprint planning if anyone would want to take on more work, I volunteered to take on more tickets thinking that the old tasks would be done quickly. Another sprint is approaching but some of my old tickets aren't yet finished. I can't stop thinking that I'll get fired soon if this continue.

[OFFICIAL] Salary Sharing thread for NEW GRADS :: September, 2021 | **MODNOTE:** Some people like these threads, some people hate them. If you hate them, that's fine, but please don't get in the way of the people who find them useful. Thanks!

This thread is for sharing recent new grad offers you've gotten or current salaries for new grads (< 2 years' experience). Friday will be the thread for people with more experience.

Please only post an offer if you're including hard numbers, but feel free to use a throwaway account if you're concerned about anonymity. You can also genericize some of your answers (e.g. "Adtech company" or "Finance startup"), or add fields if you feel something is particularly relevant.

* Education:
* Prior Experience:
    * $Internship
    * $Coop
* Company/Industry:
* Title:
* Tenure length:
* Location: 
* Salary: 
* Relocation/Signing Bonus:
* Stock and/or recurring bonuses:
* Total comp:
        
Note that while the primary purpose of these threads is obviously to share compensation info, discussion is also encouraged.

The format here is slightly unusual, so **please make sure to post under the appropriate top-level thread**, which are: US [High/Medium/Low] CoL, Western Europe, Eastern Europe, Latin America, Aus/NZ, Canada, Asia, or Other.

**If you don't work in the US, you can ignore the rest of this post.** To determine cost of living buckets, I used this site: http://www.bestplaces.net/

If the principal city of your metro is not in the reference list below, go to bestplaces, type in the name of the principal city (or city where you work in if there's no such thing), and then click "Cost of Living" in the left sidebar. The buckets are based on the Overall number: [Low: < 100], [Medium: >= 100, < 150], [High: >= 150]. (last updated Dec. 2019)
        
High CoL: NYC, LA, DC, SF Bay Area, Seattle, Boston, San Diego
        
Medium CoL: Orlando, Tampa, Philadelphia, Dallas, Phoenix, Chicago, Miami, Atlanta, Riverside, Minneapolis, Denver, Portland, Sacramento, Las Vegas, Austin, Raleigh

Low CoL: Houston, Detroit, St. Louis, Baltimore, Charlotte, San Antonio, Pittsburgh, Cincinnati, Kansas City
Got a year of spare time to learn. The catch: I don't have any internet access for most of it.

I feel guilty but is it okay to leave after a promotion? | This is a wall of text but I tried to make it as short as I could. I’m just not sure what to do and could use advice from someone who doesn’t have personal ties to me or the matter. 

I have been with my company for a few years now, it’s been great as a job. 8 months ago I received the supervisor promotion I worked towards and felt happy and confident. I had a wonderful boss, a great position, great pay, and even moreso, flexibility to do whatever I wanted outside of work to gain traction towards the future and career I actually wanted. I was in a great place in my life, most comfortable I have ever been. That lasted a good 5 months. 

Around 3-4 months ago my boss pulled me aside and let me know they were going to a different division after 8 years in the office. This left me with the choice to go for another promotion or let someone from outside of our office take over the spot. The issue with this is that my office is the only of its kind companywide. We do work that no one else would know how to do unless they learned from within. As it stood, I was the only person who knew the reports, the work, and was the most qualified. I worried that if I let someone else come in then it put my team’s morale and confidence in jeopardy and I would still be left to do the job without the pay, title, but all the responsibility while teaching someone else from the ground up. My boss had confidence that I would succeed and that I was the only viable choice. So I started the process of taking over the spot despite telling myself from the beginning that I never wanted it. The job comes with a boatload of responsibility and a new boss who is notorious for lashing out. I did the job for a month and a half before getting the actual promotion as “practice” and convinced myself this is what needed to be done for everyone’s sake despite my own feelings. I wasn’t happy but I thought I could push through it. 

I finally did the interview and got the promotion despite ny new boss feeling like I was a risk, we all knew I was the only real option, but I still felt confident and i was just nervous but would be able to do it. I pushed for a colleague that I trusted to be my right hand man and to take over my old position despite my new boss disapproving greatly. This was the best choice for everyone to succeed and I was giving myself a year until I look for something else.

Now a month later, 2 weeks into having the actual title, I hate my job with every fiber in my being. I work 60 hours a week to maintain the workload while we get fully staffed again, I am the only person in the office that is supposed to have the answers but I feel like I’m winging it half the time, I’m making mistakes, I have people hounding me from every direction. I have spent every day in tears out of stress and anxiety. I made a huge mistake and brought others into it.  My partner has spent every night and day comforting me through panic attacks and trying their best to get me through the day. My job has been on my mind 24/7, I don’t even have the energy to do anything outside of work. All the hobbies I had are unenjoyable because I either can’t focus or I’m too tired. I feel so horrible for not only doing to myself but for dragging others into it. 

An opportunity came up where I am able to interview for a job that has something to do with the industry that I actually want to work in. It’s less responsibility, it interests me, and while it would be a pay cut I do not mind. The only issue is that I’m feeling guilt for wanting to leave in the first place. For the past 3 months I’ve convinced everyone and myself that this was good and I could do it. The supervisor I set up knows virtually nothing and I fought for them to be there, and now I want to leave. My new boss would absolutely hate me.

I don’t know what to do. I feel guilty for wanting to leave because it puts everyone in a bad spot. Some people i consider friends. Everything I have done has been to keep other people happy but I am suffering for it, I even feel guilty for not being stronger. I feel selfish and weak for wanting to leave but I don’t know how long I can do this until it gets better/if it gets better.
What is wrong with me?

Most job posts out there are fake - thats why you cant get a job. | Oh there are 20 000 Jobs posted in your Region? Why then cant you get a job after sending out 100+ resumes? Because most of them are fake. The same job is posted on 5 or 10 different websites - and suddenly the 20 000 Jobs posted become 5000 in reality.

But wait - of these remaining  5000 "real" jobs half are fake posts made deliberately by the firm/corporation. And suddenly the supposed 20 000 jobs in your region shrink down to less than 2000.

Why would companies do that?

– Fake job postings exist so that employers can gauge the current  talent pool. The number of  applications can be a valuable pointer on what to pay a person for a  given job.  It also is an indicator of how easy that person is to potentially replace.

– Fake job postings exist so that companies can get a back-up for  your position and keep resumes on file. Since we already know that every  job is temporary, an enterprising employer might be stockpiling talent in case you make a sudden exit, or, are asked to make an exit.

– Fake job postings exist to make it easier for nepotism and other unfair hiring practices to occur. When the boss wants his nephew hired it’s not always cut and dry. In many organizations, the company needs  to “look outside” for suitable candidates. Searching externally for a  candidate can also satisfy any potential Equal Employment Opportunity  (EEO) mandates.  This is a quick and dirty way to appear fair.

You cannot get a job because most job openings do not exist. Sure there are some jobs that are not posted despite beeing avaliable but these are the minority. The sad truth is that most jobs that are posted out there are fake and dont exist. Coupled with the ridiculous requirements companies demand nowadays, thats the reason why people cannot find a job.
